title: Preparation of chemically competent cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"buun2","text":"Standard protocol to prepare cells for highly efficient DNA uptake using CaCl2. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":77,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Carefully suspend each pellet in 20 ml of ice-cold 100 mM CaCl 2 and incubate on ice for 1 h. 01:00:00

Inoculate a 5 ml overnight-culture in LB-medium without antibiotics (for DH5a) or with appropriate antibiotics (for other strains). Grow over night at 37 ˚C at 250 rpm. Luria-Bertani (LB) broth, makes 1L Amresco Catalog #K488
Inoculate 100 ml LB-medium with 1/100 volume of the overnight culture and incubate to an OD 600 = 0.5 - 0.6 at 37 °C and 230 rpm.
Transfer cells to two sterile 50 ml falcon tubes and incubate on ice for 20 min. From this step on, always keep cells cold. 00:20:00
Centrifuge for 5 min at 4500 rpm and 4 °C (pre-cool centrifuge) and discard the supernatant. 00:05:00
Carefully suspend each pellet in 2 ml icecold 85 mM CaCl2 containing 15% Glycerol.
Centrifuge for 5 min at 4500 rpm and 4 °C (pre-cool centrifuge) and discard the supernatant. 00:05:00

Pipette 50 - 200 µl aliquots in prepared, pre-cooled 1.5 ml tubes (work on ice), shock-freeze in liquid nitrogen or on dry ice and store at -80 °C. For DNA transformation, thaw competent cells on ice.

Required: Ice, sterile 1.5 mL tubes (label before starting), pre-cooled centrifuge 100 mM CaCl 2 , glycerol, both sterile 85 mM CaCl 2 with 15% glycerol !!! Always work under sterile conditions to avoid contamination !!!
creator: Anna Behle
